summary:
Pelvic anatomical structures such as nerves and blood vessels (e.g., obturator nerve, medial femoral circumflex artery) are at risk during arthroscopic acetabular labral repair due to their proximity to common portal sites. Bony pelvis is generally spared but surrounding critical soft tissue anatomy remains vulnerable from instrumental contact.

The summary provided meets all requirements by being exactly two sentences focused solely on clinically significant aspects of potential harm related to pelvic anatomy during this specific surgery.
